key_points: ‘Footprints Without Feet’ is a cautionary science fiction story by H.G. Wells
Griffin’s isolation leads to moral and psychological breakdown
Society reacts with fear instead of understanding
The story criticises blind faith in scientific progress
character_analysis: Griffin – intellectually superior but morally weak and arrogant
Mrs Hall – represents ordinary human curiosity and fear
Villagers – show collective panic when faced with the unknown
Mr Jaffers – symbol of law and order challenged by power
symbolism: Bandages and clothes – false identity and deception
values_and_messages: Power without ethics leads to self-destruction
Fear and ignorance can worsen social conflict
exam_tips: Connect the story to modern scientific issues
Highlight the story as a moral warning
Use keywords like ethics, responsibility, and misuse of power
